title: orchestrate command payload (the resolved action)
description: The payload of an `orchestrate` journal entry (one base envelope + per-command payload — see journal.schema.json). The driver recomputes the step cursor from the unit's artifacts and returns exactly one action; this payload is that action. The envelope carries run/orchestration/step/unit/actor/status; this payload carries the action verb and its binding (the resolved dispatch model/skills, the halt reason, or the done marker). The ordered `dispatch` entries are the run's step sequence — grouping the journal by `step` reconstructs the run.

action.description: The single resolved action for this (workflow, unit) at this point in the run.

workflow.description: Workflow (orchestration) id the action was resolved for.

reason.description: For halt: gate | blocked | unroutable | off-policy-dispatch. For error: the failure cause.

step.description: The step the action targets (the next eligible step on dispatch/halt).

actor.description: The agent the step dispatches to.

kind.description: The step's kind (author / challenge / verify / commit / gate / delegate / ...).

model.description: The model resolved deterministically by ModelRouter from the step's routing metadata.

config.description: The resolved config profile for the dispatch (deterministic / audit / exploratory / creative).

skills.description: The skills the dispatched agent loads for this step.

unit.description: Epic / Feature / Story id the action acts on.

run.description: Run id the action belongs to.

output.description: The single artifact kind the dispatched step produces.

unmet_predecessors.description: For a blocked halt: the `after` predecessors that are not yet complete.

prompt_context.description: The deterministic context handed to the dispatched agent (workflow / step / unit / instructions).
